channel.title: Strategy Sprints — Simon Severino
channel.description: Founder voice. Strategy Sprints. Sales acceleration, AI GTM.

item.title: Advisor Playbook open-sourced
item.description:
Last quarter these 5 decision frameworks saved us from two bad trades, killed two underperforming cold-email campaigns, and moved one private client from $1.3M to $3M/year in 90 days.
Open-sourced today.
The Advisor Playbook: → Kill Triggers (when to sell) → Un-Buy Review (would I buy this today?) → Temperature Gauge (when standard rules stop applying) → Task-Loss reframe (neutralize the #1 B2B AI objection) → Fungibility Test (is AI going to sort you out?)
Adapted from Howard Marks and Marc Andreessen. Running daily inside Strategy Sprints.
MIT license. Steal them.
https://github.com/SimonTheSalesBooster/advisor-playbook

item.title: Jetpack Sheet — what it is
item.description:
Most founders watched an AI workshop last quarter. How many have an agent running today? Almost none.
The gap is not the content. It's the install step.
We just shipped the Jetpack Workshop Execution Sheet.
Six rungs from "I watched the workshop" to "my first agent in production":
1. Pick the one trigger you want automated 2. Write the 5-question agent brief 3. Test it in a sandbox thread 4. Install it on your machine with screenshots and troubleshooting 5. Connect the one tool that matters 6. Run it for 72 hours, log what breaks, iterate
Step 4 is where most guides die. We wrote it with the same screenshots we use inside the paid Jetpack cohort.
This is the ladder most founders are missing.
Link in comments.

item.title: Sales forces shrinking (evergreen)
item.description:
Block cut 40% of its workforce. Stock jumped 18% the same day.
The sales floor as we knew it is over.
Recent shifts: .. Block: 10,000 to 6,000 employees. Profit guidance raised +54%. .. Salesforce: 9,000 to 5,000 support roles. Benioff on record: "I need less heads." .. Meta: $2.4M revenue per employee. Up 85% in 3 years. .. 36% of B2B companies cut their SDR team. Highest cut rate of any role.
The pattern is identical everywhere. Headcount down 30 to 50%. Revenue per employee up 15 to 85%. Markets rewarding every announcement.
The quiet version is scarier. Most companies aren't firing reps. They just stopped backfilling when reps leave.
If you run a B2B business with 5 to 10 people, the question isn't "will this reach me?" It's "which of my 3 competitors rebuilds their GTM first?"
Move first. Own the advantage.
Grab a coffee if this is where you are: calendly.com/simonseverino/coffee-with-simon
